content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.5-xhigh

## Summary

All four amendments identify real correctness or durability defects in terminal review-classification reconciliation.

## Votes

- `fr-3-bce5a1b1`: Accept
  The reconciler only selects the latest FinalReview aggregate for the run, while non-fix Review findings are explicitly deferred and earlier final-review rounds can contain accepted non-fix findings that are never queued for remediation. Limiting reconciliation to the max final-review round can silently drop committed findings.
- `fr-3-6e395bc0`: Accept
  Final-review canonicalization merges duplicate reviewer amendments into one row with multiple sources. Counting canonical rows for a threshold of 2 undercounts reviewer agreement and can suppress a proposed-bead record even when two findings support the same summary.
- `fr-3-dc280e72`: Accept
  After the target bead has been found, comment and flush failures are mutation durability failures, not harmless skips. Logging them while returning success can leave the target linkage absent and gives the normal completion path no reliable retry trigger.
- `fr-3-0587662a`: Accept
  Deduplicating with substring containment is not an exact idempotency check. An unrelated comment containing the amendment id token can falsely suppress the first legitimate covered-by reconciliation comment; the check should match the structured amendment_id field exactly.
